subtasks.description: Generate SQL query to calculate current month spend and 3-month rolling average by department/vendor, compute variance percentage, and return spend metrics for anomaly detection

Reconcile current spend against 3-month baseline, identify statistical anomalies, classify each anomaly as normal_variance (±15%), concerning (15-30%), or critical (>30%), and return ranked anomaly list with business context

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen (mid tier) to compute current vs. 3-month rolling average and variance—requires multi-period aggregation and joins across time windows, (2) cross_recon (mid tier) to classify anomalies against thresholds and surface business signals. The classification step is part of reconciliation, not a separate policy_action, since it is algorithmic thresholding not governance. Both mid tier because they involve moderate aggregation/windowing (SQL) and statistical reasoning (cross_recon) but no frontier-level complexity like ML inference or extreme cardinality.

output.sql: WITH monthly_spend AS (SELECT vendor_name, DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) AS month, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend FROM invoice_facts WHERE invoice_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY vendor_name, DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date)), with_rolling AS (SELECT vendor_name, month, total_spend, AVG(total_spend) OVER (PARTITION BY vendor_name ORDER BY month ROWS BETWEEN 3 PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING) AS rolling_3mo_avg FROM monthly_spend), with_variance AS (SELECT vendor_name, month, total_spend, rolling_3mo_avg, CASE WHEN rolling_3mo_avg IS NULL OR rolling_3mo_avg = 0 THEN NULL ELSE ((total_spend - rolling_3mo_avg) / rolling_3mo_avg) * 100 END AS variance_pct FROM with_rolling) SELECT vendor_name, month, total_spend, rolling_3mo_avg, variance_pct, CASE WHEN variance_pct IS NULL THEN 'insufficient_history' WHEN ABS(variance_pct) <= 20 THEN 'normal_variance' WHEN ABS(variance_pct) <= 50 THEN 'concerning' ELSE 'critical' END AS anomaly_classification FROM with_variance ORDER BY month DESC, ABS(variance_pct) DESC NULLS LAST
